lede: A look at February 21 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Corbin Bleu and Robert Mugabe, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Suez Canal
events.desc: The last Israeli soldiers leave the west bank of the Suez Canal pursuant to a truce with Egypt.

events.title: Richard Nixon
events.desc: United States President Richard Nixon visits China to normalize Sino-American relations.

events.title: Soviet Union
events.desc: The Soviet uncrewed spaceship Luna 20 lands on the Moon.

events.title: Bengali language movement
events.desc: The Bengali language movement protests occur at the University of Dhaka in East Pakistan (now Bangladesh).

events.title: Winston Churchill
events.desc: The British government, under Winston Churchill, abolishes identity cards in the UK to "set the people free".

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: During the Battle of Iwo Jima, Japanese kamikaze planes sink the escort carrier USS Bismarck Sea and damage the USS Saratoga.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: In France, the Battle of Verdun begins.

events.title: American Civil War
events.desc: American Civil War: Battle of Valverde is fought near Fort Craig in New Mexico Territory.

events.title: Karl Marx
events.desc: Karl Marx and Friedrich Engels publish The Communist Manifesto.

events.title: Cherokee Phoenix
events.desc: Initial issue of the Cherokee Phoenix is the first periodical to use the Cherokee syllabary invented by Sequoyah.

births.name: Corbin Bleu
births.line: American actor, model, dancer, film producer and singer-songwriter

births.name: Robert Mugabe
births.line: Zimbabwean educator and politician, 2nd President of Zimbabwe

deaths.text: Gertrude B. Elion, American biochemist and pharmacologist, Nobel Prize laureate
Mikhail Sholokhov, Russian novelist and short story writer, Nobel Prize laureate
